Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Party-Affiliated Witnesses Of Deft To Attend And Testify At Deposition And Produce For Inspection Documents Described In Depositio Nnotice And For Imposing Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-02-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Daniel D. McGee, Esq., Law Offices, 916-442-7556, for Defendant and moving party
Chad D. Greeson, Esq., from Archer Norris, 925-930-6600, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF SCHINDLER ELEVATOR CORPORATION'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions Of Party-Affiliated Witnesses Of Defendant 490 Post Street LLC To Attend And Testify At Deposition And Produce For Inspection Documents Described In Deposition Notice And For Imposing Monetary Sanctions IS
Granted. All four persons must be made available for deposition prior to December 9, 2011. Defendant's conduct in not serving any objections to the notice and then sending an email the day before the depositions were noticed is the kind of gamesmanship that the discovery statutes and the appellate authority construing them have condemned. The fact that the deposition notice (apparently inaccurately) referred to the deponents as 'members' of defendant and has the wrong first name of one of the deponents does not justify defendant's conduct, especially where, as here, there is no evidence disputing plaintiff's contention that all four deponents are managing agents or officers of defendant or otherwise sufficiently affiliated with defendant to be deemed party-affiliated witnesses. The failure to serve timely objections waives any objections to the taking of the depositions, but not to questions posed at the depositions. All documents sought in the deposition notice must be produced at the depositions, except those for which a privilege is asserted. As to the documents withheld on grounds of privilege, a privilege log is required, except for documents reflecting communications with Mr. Bernstein after he was retained to defend this lawsuit. Sanctions of $907.50 are awarded in favor of plaintiff and against defendant and must be paid by December 31, 2011. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold Kahn, not reported.
